message:
feat(markdown): add GFM auto-detection and streaming rendering support

Enhance the markdown rendering system with two major capabilities:

1. GFM Auto-Detection (`is_likely_markdown`):
   - Heuristic function to detect if text is likely GitHub-Flavored Markdown
   - Useful for determining whether to render raw text vs formatted markdown
   - Checks for common GFM patterns (headers, lists, code blocks, links, etc.)

2. Streaming/Fragment Rendering (`render_streaming`):
   - Support for incremental rendering of partial markdown content
   - Critical for LLM response display where text arrives token-by-token
   - Gracefully handles incomplete syntax (unclosed code blocks, partial links)
   - Maintains visual consistency as content streams in

Demo showcase updates:
   - New comprehensive demo content simulating LLM-generated markdown
   - Demonstrates quantum computing explanation with LaTeX math, tables,
     code blocks, task lists, and complex nested structures
   - Showcases real-world formatting that LLMs produce

This enables ftui applications to render AI assistant output in real-time
with proper syntax highlighting and formatting as the response streams in.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>
